title: Change your password from settings
summary: Let people who sign in with a username and password update that password right from their settings page, with clear success and error messages. People who sign in through an outside login service instead see a short note telling them to manage their password there.
motivation: Let local-auth users change their password from settings while keeping OIDC accounts pointed at their identity provider.

included: POST /api/settings/change-password remains the backend password-change contract
password-change business rules move from routers/settings.py into the settings service module
SettingsView gains a local-account password form with success and error states
OIDC users see a non-editable identity-provider message instead of a password form
excluded: password reset by email, password strength meters, and server-side logout of other sessions
OIDC password management links unless the backend already exposes one
account deletion and export UI; those are separate binders
new password validation rules beyond the backend PasswordChangeRequest contract

design_facts.stack: Mixed Vue 3/TypeScript/Vite frontend plus FastAPI/Python 3.13/SQLAlchemy async/PostgreSQL backend; DTCG design tokens; no router or Pinia.

token_manifest.system: W3C DTCG JSON built with Style Dictionary into CSS custom properties.
token_manifest.consumption_rule: New component CSS consumes semantic custom properties only; primitives are not used in new code.

work_items.title: Let the server change your password safely
work_items.summary: Move the rules for changing a password into a shared service so the server checks the current password, blocks reusing the same one, and still tells outside-login users to manage their password with their provider.

exposes.function: async change_password(db: AsyncSession, current_user: User, request: PasswordChangeRequest) -> MessageResponse

behavior: local users must provide the correct current password
OIDC users or users without a local password_hash receive the existing 400 identity-provider message
new password cannot verify against the current password hash
routers/settings.py remains dependency wiring plus one service call

oracle.command: make test-backend-lint && make test-backend-format && make test-backend-types && cd backend && uv run pytest tests/integration/test_settings.py::TestChangePassword tests/integration/test_settings.py::TestChangePasswordOIDC tests/unit/test_settings_schemas.py::TestPasswordChangeRequest -v
assertions: Local password change success, wrong-current-password, same-password, auth-required, and validation cases still pass.
OIDC users still receive the identity-provider-only password-change response.
The changed code has typed functions, no bare except, no secret logging, and no raw password assignment.

work_items.title: Change your password on the settings page
work_items.summary: Give password-based users an on-screen form to enter their current and new password and confirm it, showing a success note when it works and clear errors when it does not, while outside-login users see a message instead of a form.

component_map.mapping: Custom Vue SFC or SettingsView subsection
component_map.notes: Use native password inputs and submit button; no dependency for visibility toggles unless existing icons are reused.

behavior: local users see current password, new password, and confirm new password inputs
client blocks only mismatched confirmation and obviously too-short new passwords; backend remains final authority
submit sends current_password and new_password exactly as typed
success clears password fields and renders a confirmation message
API errors render inline without clearing account-summary state
OIDC users do not see a writable password form

signals: explicit-open-question:client-side password checks are deliberately minimal and backend-owned

oracle.command: make test-ui-lint && cd ui && npm run test -- src/services/settings.test.ts src/features/settings/useSettings.test.ts src/features/settings/SettingsView.test.ts
assertions: Local-account form posts current_password and new_password to /api/settings/change-password with bearer auth.
OIDC account info renders the identity-provider message and no password inputs.
Mismatched confirmation is blocked on the client, while backend API errors are shown inline and fields are not logged or exposed.
